front: What is the primary purpose of a Burndown Chart in Agile project management?
back: A Burndown Chart is a visual tool used to track the amount of work completed versus the amount of work remaining over time. It helps the team monitor progress toward a sprint goal and predict if they are on track to finish all planned work.

front: What is a 'Task Board' (or Kanban Board) and what are its typical columns?
back: A Task Board is a physical or digital visualization tool that provides a real-time overview of the status of all work items in a sprint. Typical columns include 'To Do', 'In Progress', and 'Done' (or 'Completed').

front: Define what a 'User Story' is in Agile methodologies.
back: A User Story is a simple, informal, natural language description of a software feature from the perspective of the end-user. It follows the general template: 'As a [type of user], I want [some goal] so that [some reason].'

front: What is the 'Definition of Done' (DoD) and why is it a critical Agile tool?
back: The Definition of Done is a shared understanding within the team of what it means for a work item (like a user story) to be considered complete. It is a checklist of criteria that must be met, ensuring quality and consistency. It prevents ambiguity and 'almost done' work.

front: What is the main activity and goal of a daily Scrum (or Stand-up) meeting?
back: The daily Scrum is a short, time-boxed (usually 15 minutes) meeting for the development team to synchronize activities. Each member answers: What did I do yesterday? What will I do today? Are there any impediments? The goal is to surface problems and plan the next 24 hours.
